Document:
# MKCs
An MKC (short for Markov Knowledge Chassis) is a type of sapient robot, originally designed as disposable and easily repairable laborers by a now-defunct corporate power.
They are considered [color=yellow]independent individuals[/color], meaning [color=red]they are not typically guided by any laws of robotics[/color]. MKCs cannot be hacked by emags and are not affected by ion storms because they do not have to follow any predefined directives in their system.
## Species Traits
MKCs are silicon-based beings, having a positronic brain similar to cyborgs and a mechanical chassis. This means that they: - [color=red]Cannot be healed by normal means at the Medbay;[/color] see the section on MKC Repair for more details - Cannot ingest any food or drink - Do not need to breathe - Are not affected by most reagents; see the section on the Fluid Regulator for more details - Cannot take Poison, Asphyxiation, or Cellular damage from any source - Do not rot after death - Start overheating and taking Heat damage while in critical condition instead of asphyxiating
They also have the following miscellaneous traits, particular to MKCs: - Damage Resistances: Cold -30% - Damage Weaknesses: Shock +30% - Greatly increased tolerance for both heat and cold, but less effective thermal regulation - Take exclusively Heat damage from Barotrauma instead of Blunt and Heat - Have a bloodstream of oil - Are stunned and disoriented by EMP effects
## Power Core
Instead of sustaining themselves on food or drink, MKCs have an internal power core that they must keep charged. If this battery gets too low, the MKC's movements will become more sluggish until they can recharge.
The internal battery of an MKC can be recharged by [color=yellow]using the verb in the right-click menu[/color] of any item or structure with an internal battery. Common examples include power cells and APCs, but you can also use things like energy weapons in a pinch.
## MKC Repair Instead of the Medbay, the best place for MKCs to have their injuries treated is the [color=yellow]Robotics Lab.[/color] Basic repair supplies can also usually be found in [color=yellow]Engineering,[/color] but they lack the advanced resources and lathes of Robotics.
These places will have unique items capable of healing MKCs. These are:
Heals all forms of Brute damage.
Heals Burn damage, plus minor Caustic damage.
Heals Shock damage.
Heals Cold damage.
Stops bleeding, just like with biologicals!
Restores blood and heals Bloodloss damage.
MKCs in critical condition [color=red]cannot be revived by standard defibrillators,[/color] and must instead be resuscitated by these specialized units.
Nanites are a unique category of reagent that [color=yellow]only affects MKCs.[/color] They can be administered to treat unusual forms of damage like radiation and blindness.
## Metabolisms and the Fluid Regulator
Most reagents cannot affect MKCs and are not processed by their bodies. Generally, the only reagents that can be processed by MKCs are those with [color=yellow]caustic, radioactive, thermodynamic, electromagnetic, or esoteric properties,[/color] as well as [color=yellow]nanites[/color] as described above.
If any other reagent enters an MKC's body, a special organ called the fluid regulator will activate. This begins a very painful and uncomfortable process that eventually results in the violent expulsion of excess fluid from an MKC's body, leaving them in a puddle on the ground.
Doing this will also deal Piercing damage and have other nasty effects on the MKC proportionate to the amount of fluid released!
## Naming Traditions MKCs have a serial designation consisting of two parts: A short alphanumeric portion, typically a word or shortened version thereof, and a three-digit numerical portion.
Examples: - CROW-550 - HNTR-241 - EYE-073
